---
title: "HW 4 - Bivariate Visualizations"
output: 
  flexdashboard::flex_dashboard:
    orientation: columns
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
library(flexdashboard)
library(flexdashboard)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(readxl)
library(readr)
library(ggrepel)
library(vcd)
library(glue)
library(scales)
```


```{r}
setwd("C:\\Users\\bella\\OneDrive\\UMass\\DACSS_690V\\HW_4")
```
```{r}
rm(list = ls())
library(readxl)
arrests <- read_excel("C:/Users/bella/OneDrive/UMass/DACSS_690V/HW_4/Mass_Arrests.xlsx")
sheet2 <- read_excel("C:/Users/bella/OneDrive/UMass/DACSS_690V/HW_4/Mass_Arrests.xlsx", sheet = 2)
```

## Column {data-width="500"}

### Cat-Cat

```{r}
# contingency table of counts
# independent variable in the columns
# dependent variable in the rows
(ArrestTypeSex = table(arrests$Sex, arrests$`Arrest Type`))
```
```{r}
library(magrittr) # for %>%
(ArrestTypeSex_mgCol=prop.table(ArrestTypeSex,
                            margin = 2)%>%round(.,3))
```

```{r}
SexArrestType = table(arrests$Sex, arrests$`Arrest Type`)
SexArrestType_mgCol = prop.table(SexArrestType,
                            margin = 2) %>% round(., 3)
SexArrestType_mgCol


#data frame from the contingency table
ArrestTypeSexDF=as.data.frame(ArrestTypeSex)
names(ArrestTypeSexDF)=c("Sex","Arrest_Type","counts")

#percentages:
ArrestTypeSexDF$pctCol=100*as.data.frame(ArrestTypeSex_mgCol)[,3]
ArrestTypeSexDF

```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
```

```{r}
# stacked bar chart
base3 = ggplot(data = ArrestTypeSexDF,
               aes(x = Arrest_Type, y = pctCol,
                   fill = reorder(Sex, pctCol)))

barStacked = base3 + geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = 'stack')

barStacked_annot = barStacked +
  geom_text(size = 4,
            color = ifelse(ArrestTypeSexDF$pctCol < 25, "white", "black"),
            position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5),
            aes(label = paste0(pctCol, '%')))

barStacked_final = barStacked_annot + scale_fill_viridis_d(option = "cividis") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Arrest Type Distribution by Sex",
       subtitle = "Massachusetts Arrest Data",
       x = "Arrest Type",
       y = "",
       fill = "Sex",
       caption = "Source: Mass Arrests Dataset") +
  theme(axis.text.y = element_blank())

barStacked_final
```


```{r}
covid <- read.csv("C:\\Users\\bella\\OneDrive\\UMass\\DACSS_690V\\HW_4\\covid-19.csv")
```

## Column {data-width="500"}

### Num-Num: Scatterplot

```{r}
covid_country <- aggregate(data = covid,
                           cbind(daily_deaths_mean, daily_cases_mean) ~ Entity,
                           sum)
covid_country <- covid_country[covid_country$daily_deaths_mean > 0 &
                                covid_country$daily_cases_mean > 0, ]

# Scatter plot
base <- ggplot(covid_country)
vars <- base + aes(x = daily_cases_mean, y = daily_deaths_mean)
geoms <- vars + geom_point()
scat_text <- geoms + geom_text_repel(aes(label = Entity), size = 2)
geoms_d <- vars + stat_density_2d(aes(fill = after_stat(density)),
                                   geom = "raster",
                                   contour = FALSE)
scatDens <- geoms_d +
  geom_text_repel(aes(label = Entity),
                  min.segment.length = 0,
                  color = "white",
                  fontface = "bold",
                  bg.color = "black",
                  bg.r = .15,
                  size = 3) +
  scale_x_continuous(expand = c(0, 0), 
                     labels = scales::comma) +  
  scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 0),
                     labels = scales::comma) +  
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "cividis", guide = "none") +
  theme_light() +
  labs(title = "As Daily Cases Rose, Daily Deaths Followed",
       subtitle = "Each dot represents a country. Brighter areas have more clustered countries.",
       x = "Average Daily Cases",
       y = "Average Daily Deaths",
       caption = "Source: COVID-19 Global Dataset\nNote: Only countries with recorded cases and deaths are shown.") +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10, color = "gray40"),
        plot.caption = element_text(hjust = 0, color = "gray40"),
        axis.title = element_text(size = 11))

scatDens
```

## Column {data-width="500"}

### Num-Cat 
```{r}
arrests_age <- arrests[arrests$Age > 0, ]

# Setup
baseBox <- ggplot(data = arrests_age,
                  aes(y = Age))

boxPlot <- baseBox +
  geom_boxplot(aes(x = reorder(`Arrest Type`, Age, median))) +
  coord_flip() +
  theme_light() +
  labs(title = "Age Varies by Arrest Type",
       x = "Arrest Type",
       y = "Age",
       caption = "Source: Mass Arrests Dataset")

# plot 2
vioBox <- baseBox +
  geom_violin(aes(x = `Arrest Type`),
              fill = "orange", trim = FALSE) +
  geom_boxplot(aes(x = `Arrest Type`),
               fill = NA, color = "red", width = 0.1) +
  coord_flip() +
  theme_light() +
  labs(title = "Age Distribution Varies Across Arrest Types",
       subtitle = "Orange = distribution shape, Red = median and spread",
       x = "Arrest Type",
       y = "Age",
       caption = "Source: Mass Arrests Dataset")

vioBox

# stat test
kruskal_test <- kruskal.test(Age ~ `Arrest Type`, data = arrests_age)

glue("
Kruskal-Wallis Test Results
χ²({kruskal_test$parameter}) = {round(kruskal_test$statistic, 1)}, p < {ifelse(kruskal_test$p.value < 0.001, '0.001', round(kruskal_test$p.value, 3))}
")
```

## Column {data-width="500"}

### Improved Multivariate Plot


```{r}
library(readxl)
library(reshape2)
library(ggpubr)

# data
download.file("https://github.com/DACSS-Visual/tabular_multivar/raw/main/data/safeCities_ecoUI.xlsx",
              destfile = "safeCities_ecoUI.xlsx", mode = "wb")

safeALL <- read_excel("safeCities_ecoUI.xlsx")

drop <- c("Overall_Score")
keep <- setdiff(names(safeALL), drop)
safe <- safeALL[, keep]

# reshape to long format
safeL <- melt(safe, id.vars = 'city')

# original
original <- ggplot(data = safeL,
                   aes(x = variable, y = city)) +
  geom_tile(aes(fill = value)) +
  labs(title = "Original Plot", x = "", y = "") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1, size = 5),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 5))

# improved
improved <- ggplot(data = safeL) +
  aes(x = reorder(variable, value, FUN = median),
      y = reorder(city, value, FUN = median)) +
  geom_tile(aes(fill = value)) +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "cividis", name = "Score") +
  labs(title = "Safety and Sustainability Scores by City and Indicator",
       subtitle = "Safety Ordered by median score",
       x = "", y = "",
       caption = "Source: Safe Cities & Eco UI Index") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_blank(),  
        axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 7),
        plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 9, color = "gray40"))
ggarrange(original, improved, ncol = 2, widths = c(1, 1.3))
```
